sections.paragraph:
δεσμοφύλαξ, ακος, ὁ (s. δέσμος and φύλαξ; Lucian, Tox. 30; Artem. 3, 60; Vett. Val. 68, 26; Cass. Dio 76, 10, 3; pap since III b.c. [Mayser 467], also BGU 1138, 12ff; TestJos 2:3; Jos., Ant. 2, 61; AcThom 118 [Aa II/2, 228, 15] al. ἀρχιδεσμοφύλαξ Gen 39:21–23; 41:10 v.l.) jailer, keeper of a prison Ac 16:23, 27, 36.—M-M.

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mayser Mayser = EM., Grammatik der griechischen Papyri aus der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

TestJos TestJos = Testament of Joseph, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

AcThom AcThom = Acts of Thomas—List 5

Aa Aa = Acta apostolorum apocrypha—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 219
